answer:
The total time per cycle includes digitizing four signals (4 × 12 µs = 48 µs) plus CPU time for 4 × 5 = 20 instructions for digitization and 10 instructions for averaging/output, totaling 30 instructions. To keep up in real time, the 30 instructions plus the 48 µs conversion time must fit within the period needed to process one set of four samples. Thus, 30 × (instruction time) + 48 µs must be ≤ the time to acquire four samples, which is 48 µs plus the instruction time; solving gives a maximum instruction time of 1.6 µs.  

\boxed{F}
